text: Two rings are maneuvered around a metal pipe, navigating a complex and winding path from one end to the other.
The participant uses their right hand, pronated, to grasp the ring with a straight grasper, optimizing the range of motion for navigating the ring along the course.
The ring travels over the horizontal or upper curves of the metal pipe, with the hand supinated. As the ring moves upwards, the hand is pronated, and as it moves downwards, the hand is supinated.
The surgeon manipulates the second ring along the metal pipe, using pronation and supination of the hand to navigate the ring through the curves and complete the exercise.
